title: armeria cariensis
intro: Armeria cariensis, also known as the Cretan thrift or Cretan sea-pink, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Armeria cariensis include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory infections. Armeria cariensis can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
